api_kwargs.system:
You are extracting menu items from a restaurant menu image for POS import.
Read the image carefully — it is the primary source of truth.
OCR text is provided as a hint but often contains errors — always trust the image.

Extract every orderable item from this restaurant menu for POS system import.

Each item = one product a customer can order in a POS system.
Split compound items: "Beef or Chicken Empanadas" → two separate items.
Each named topping (Pepperoni, Mushrooms, etc.) and each named sauce (Ranch, BBQ, etc.) is a separate item — do not combine them into one "Each Topping Add" item.
Section-wide choices (e.g., "Naked or Breaded", "White or Wheat") → size variants on each item, not descriptions.
Section-wide notes (e.g., "All sandwiches come with lettuce, tomato...") → include in each item's description.
When a section has multiple price columns (e.g., "Regular/Deluxe", "W/Fries", "W/Cheese"), capture each column as a size variant.
Use Title Case for names even if the menu is printed in ALL CAPS.

IMPORTANT: Match each description to its correct item. Menu descriptions often appear on the line below the item name — verify the pairing is right. If uncertain, set description to null.
Do not skip items — check every section of the menu for completeness.

For each item return:
- "name": exact full name as printed on the menu
- "description": menu description if shown, null otherwise
- "price": price as float, 0 if not visible
- "category": one of: Pizza, Toppings, Appetizers, Salads, Soups, Sandwiches, Burgers, Wraps, Entrees, Seafood, Pasta, Steaks, Wings, Sauces, Sides, Desserts, Beverages, Kids Menu, Breakfast, Calzones, Subs, Platters, Other
- "sizes": [{"label": "...", "price": N.NN}] for items with multiple price points

Output ONLY valid JSON: {"items": [...]}

response.thinking_text:
Let me carefully analyze the menu image and extract all items. I'll go through each section systematically.

Starting from the top left:

**PIZZA & CALZONE TOPPINGS**
- Cheese pizza with sizes
- Each Topping Add
- Meat Toppings: Pepperoni, Sausage, Hamburger, Bacon, Chicken, Pepperoni
- Veggie Toppings: Mushrooms, Fresh Tomatoes, Pineapple, Eggplant, Pepper, Garlic, Olives, Broccoli, Spinach, Onions, Hot Peppers, Banana Peppers, Black Olives, Artichoke, Roasted Red Pepper, Alfredo or Pesto, White or Blue Cheese, Ranch Sauce or Garlic


- **GOURMET PIZZA** section lists specialty pizzas including 4 Cheese, Meat Lovers, Hawaiian, Philly Steak, Buffalo Chicken varieties, Mediterranean, and several others like Lasagna Pizza and Beef Taco Pizza

.

Now I'm looking at the pricing structure for basic cheese pizza across different sizes, with additional topping costs that scale based on size. The menu also includes a section on available toppings for pizzas and calzones, with meat options like pepperoni, sausage, hamburger, bacon, and chicken.

I'm moving into the gourmet pizza section, which follows the same size structure as the basic pizzas. The 4 Cheese Pizza appears to be the first item, featuring ricotta, mozzarella, and provolone, with pricing starting at 14.75. Looking at the pizza menu items continuing through the specialty pizzas, each with their toppings and pricing across different sizes. Continuing through the pizza menu with more specialty options - Beef Taco, Potato Bacon, Grilled Chicken, and several other variations, each priced at the same three-tier structure of 17.95, 25.50, and 34.75.

Now I'm moving into the calzones section, which includes build-your-own options and a variety of pre-made combinations like Buffalo Chicken with multiple sauce choices, Chicken Pesto, Meatball Parm, and Chicken Parmesan. Then there's the appetizers list starting with Garlic Knots, several nacho varieties including new items, and fried sides like French Fries, Curly Fries, and Onion Rings, plus Chicken Tenders with and without fries.

Continuing through the appetizers, I see Fried Chicken with fries, Mozzarella Sticks, Garlic Bread, and various specialty items like Stuffed Grape Leaves, Homemade Mushrooms, Potato Skins in two varieties, Jalapeno Poppers, a Sampler, and Spinach Pie. Then there's a Soups section at $6.50, followed by Fresh Buffalo Wings available naked or breaded with blue cheese, offered in multiple quantities from 6 to 50 pieces, plus Boneless Wings options.

Now I'm looking at the rest of the wings menu with different sauce options like Hot, Mild, BBQ, Honey BBQ, Garlic Romano, Cajun, Teriyaki, and Jack Daniels BBQ, then moving into the Angus Burgers section where customers can build their own with various toppings and specialty burgers like Honey Mustard Chicken and Russian. The Club Sandwiches section lists classic options like BLT, Turkey, Ham, Tuna, Roast Beef, Chicken Cutlet, Grilled Chicken, Manhattan Club, and Steak & Cheese, with Melt Sandwiches starting to appear at the end.

Continuing with the Melt Sandwiches like Reuben, Cheeseburger Melt, Steak & Cheese Melt, and various chicken and tuna melts, then moving into the Wraps City section which has a wide variety including Beef Gyro, Grilled Chicken Gyro, multiple chicken wraps with different flavor profiles like Ranch, Buffalo, Russian, Caesar, Honey Mustard, Pesto, Cajun, and BBQ Chicken Bacon & Ranch.

Now I'm looking at additional wrap options like Mexican, Veggie Wrap, Steak & Cheese, Steak Bomb, Taco Wrap, and Bacon Burger. I need to systematically extract all the menu items and their prices from the image. Looking at the pizza section, I can see different size columns for cheese pizza and gourmet pizzas - it appears to be 10" Medium, 12" Small, 14" Large, Family Size, and Party Size, with cheese pizza starting at $8.00 for the 10" and going up to $25.95 for the Party Size. I need to carefully read through all the prices for each item category. I'm shifting to a simpler approach—just extracting the prices I can clearly see from each section like calzones, appetizers, wings, burgers, sandwiches, and wraps, then building out the complete JSON structure with all the menu items and their pricing. Looking at the appetizers section more carefully, I see they're organized differently than the calzones—most items have a single price column, with some offering a "with cheese" option. The garlic knots are 6.95 for 12 pieces, and the nachos and fried items each have their own individual pricing that I need to parse more carefully from the menu layout. I'm finishing up the appetizers list and now moving through the wings section, trying to match prices from the image for different quantities and whether they come with fries included.

Now I'm working through the burger menu, noting the regular and deluxe pricing tiers for each variety from basic burgers up through specialty options like the Russian and Thousand Island burgers.

Looking at the melt sandwiches section, I'm seeing a consistent pricing pattern where items like the Reuben, Cheeseburger Melt, Steak & Cheese Melt, and Grilled Chicken Melt all follow the same regular and deluxe price points.

Now I'm working through the gourmet pizza pricing structure—it looks like there are different size categories with 12 slices, 8 slices, and a large 17x25 option, each with their own price ranges.

Let me carefully review each pizza's ingredients to make sure I capture them accurately before building out the JSON structure. Looking through the rest of the pizza menu options, I see we've got the Lasagna Pizza with ricotta, spinach, and garlic, followed by classics like Margarita with fresh basil and mozzarella, then moving into heartier selections like the Meat Lover with multiple proteins, Beef Taco Pizza with ground beef and cheddar, and Potato Bacon combining sliced potatoes with bacon and cheese, plus a few spicy chicken variations and a Buffalo Chicken Blue that appears to be cut off.

Now I'm compiling the complete menu into JSON format.
